description:
Automated translation (refer to source for accuracy)

On Monday, May 11, Fujishima Raiki, a men's hockey player for Japan's national team based at ALDER Hanno, and related staff visited City Hall to report their qualification for the 16th FIH Men's Hockey World Cup.

The World Cup will be held jointly in Amsterdam, Netherlands, and Waterloo, Belgium, from August 15 to 30, 2026 (Reiwa 8), for 16 days.

Japan's men's hockey team finished in third place at the World Cup final qualifying tournament held in March, securing their spot in the World Cup.

Fujishima is currently serving as captain of Japan's men's hockey national team, leading the team with distinction.

We heartily honor Fujishima's achievements and look forward to continued success that will further promote Hanno as a "hockey town."

Source (Japanese): https://www.city.hanno.lg.jp/shiseijoho/shichoshitsu/shichoblog/shisei2026/shisei202605/13573.html
summary: 📝 Mayor: Courtesy visit from Fujishima Raiki, men's hockey Japan national team player
